title: KryptoniteUserStory
description: Schema for a fully specified user story or spike in the kryptonite workflow. Every item must conform to this schema before the spec can be generated.

type.description: feature = standard implementation story. spike = research task that produces knowledge/decisions to inform other stories. Spikes must run before the stories that depend on them.

party.description: The actor/role this story belongs to (must match a defined party from Phase 4). For spikes, use 'system' or the party most relevant to the research.

repo.description: Name of the repository this story belongs to (must match a `name` in the project's repos.json). Cross-repo stories are split into per-repo sub-stories during Phase 8 (see Cross-Repo Story Auto-Split).

as_a.description: The actor role, e.g. 'admin'. For spikes: 'developer' or 'team'.

i_want.description: The action/capability desired. For spikes: the research question.

so_that.description: The motivation/business value. For spikes: what decision this unblocks.

acceptance_criteria.description: Testable conditions. For features: verifiable behaviors. For spikes: questions that must be answered.

description.description: What must be true for this item to pass.

method.description: How to verify this DOD item. curl = HTTP request check. chrome_mcp = browser-based verification. test_suite = run test command. file_exists = check a file was produced (for spikes).

oneOf.description: The exact command or URL to run for verification. Use ${APP_URL} as base URL placeholder. e.g. 'curl -s -o /dev/null -w %{http_code} ${APP_URL}/api/users' or 'npm test -- --grep US-001'
Structured steps for chrome_mcp validation. Each step is an action object.

expect.description: Expected result. e.g. '201', 'PASS', 'file exists', or a pattern to match.

definition_of_done.description: Concrete, verifiable items that define completion. Each item includes HOW to validate it automatically. If an item cannot be validated via curl, chrome_mcp, test_suite, or file_exists, it must be rewritten until it can.

priority.description: Business priority. Critical = blocks other work or is a launch requirement. High = important for MVP. Medium = desirable. Low = nice to have.

dependencies.description: List of story IDs that must be completed before this story can be implemented. Spikes that inform a story must be listed as dependencies. Empty array if no dependencies.

estimated_complexity.description: Implementation complexity estimate. Trivial = config change or one-liner. Simple = single file, clear path. Moderate = multiple files, some decisions. Complex = cross-cutting, multiple components. Very complex = architectural changes or novel problems.

has_mock.description: Whether this story has a UI component that needs a visual mockup. Set to true during Phase 5 for stories with user-facing UI. Mock files are stored in the epic's mocks/ directory.

mock_approved.description: Whether the mock for this story has been approved by the user.

amended.description: Whether this story was modified after plan approval. Set to true when acceptance_criteria or definition_of_done are changed during execution.

changed_at.description: ISO timestamp of when the amendment was made

amendment_history.description: History of amendments made to this story after plan approval.
